patterns_discovered.description: Every model includes organization FK and critical rule that all queries MUST filter by organization_id
Both Zone and Table inherit SoftDeleteMixin and use soft_delete() method instead of physical deletion
All models use UUID fields as primary keys for global uniqueness instead of auto-incrementing integers
Comprehensive docstrings with critical rules, attributes, usage examples, and gotchas
Implementation of both SoftDeleteManager (default, excludes soft-deleted) and all_objects (includes all)
Both models include JSONField for flexible additional settings with getter/setter methods
Multiple composite indexes on (organization, other_fields) to optimize tenant isolation queries

gotchas_discovered.description: Critical rule states 'NEVER call delete() directly' - must use soft_delete() method. This is emphasized in docstrings and documented in module-level comments
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Model provides soft_delete() method inherited from SoftDeleteMixin

gotchas_discovered.description: Every single query MUST include organization filtering for multi-tenant isolation. This is a critical rule repeated throughout documentation
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use SoftDeleteManager which enforces this pattern; documented in usage examples

gotchas_discovered.description: Zone slugs are unique within an organization (not globally), and same likely applies to Table slugs
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Unique constraints defined with unique_together using (organization, slug)

gotchas_discovered.description: Zone has table_count, available_table_count, occupied_table_count properties that query related tables. Table's is_available property depends on zone status
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Properties use select_related/prefetch_related patterns; N+1 queries possible if not careful

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Foreign key relationships and cascade delete
gotchas_discovered.description: All FK relationships use on_delete=models.CASCADE, which means deleting an Organization or Zone will cascade. However, soft_delete pattern complicates this
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Soft delete pattern prevents actual deletions; CASCADE still applies to soft_delete() calls

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive Zone and Table models for restaurant layout management with full multi-tenant isolation, soft delete support, and extensive documentation
key_achievements: Implemented Zone model with 14 fields covering section management, capacity, and characteristics
Implemented Table model with 16 fields covering physical table properties, status, and positioning
Integrated SoftDeleteMixin and TimeStampedMixin for data preservation and audit trails
Established multi-tenant isolation pattern with organization FK on both models
Created dual manager pattern (SoftDeleteManager + all_objects) for flexible querying
Added rich property methods for table counting and status queries
Implemented settings management with get_setting/set_setting helper methods
Created database indexes for query optimization on common filter combinations
Provided comprehensive documentation with critical rules, usage examples, and gotchas

recommendations.recommendation: Create a migration guide document explaining the soft delete pattern and multi-tenant isolation requirements for developers using these models
Create comprehensive test cases covering: soft delete behavior, organization isolation, cascade scenarios, and all custom methods
Consider adding composite index on (organization, is_active, deleted_at) for common 'active zones/tables' queries
Add clean() method validation to ensure min_capacity <= capacity for Table model
Document when to use select_related('organization', 'zone') and prefetch_related('tables') to avoid N+1 queries
Create custom QuerySet methods like get_active(), get_by_zone(), filter_by_capacity() to encourage correct multi-tenant patterns
Module docstring indicates QRCode, QRScan, Order, OrderItem, and ServiceRequest models to be added in subsequent subtasks - ensure they follow same patterns
